trajectory.command: mkdir -p test_data && cp test_eval_config.json test_data/eval_config.json && python train.py && python predict.py --data_path test_data --checkpoint checkpoints --output output.json && cat output.json

trajectory.description: Observation: Starting with empty workspace, first iteration as required. Analysis: Must implement full pipeline with Qlib + Alpha158 + LGBModel for stock IC prediction baseline, using few boosting rounds for speed and immediate eval submission to measure time. Decision: Write train.py (LGB with 50 rounds, train/valid split), predict.py (recreates handler for test period, loads booster, computes daily IC/rankIC and required JSON metrics), test config; run training+prediction test then submit_eval on checkpoints.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline LGB (50 rounds) achieved ic_mean=0.0281, rank_ic_mean=0.0081 on test (484 days). Training+eval pipeline works (data loading ~43s, total iter ~120s). Analysis: Low boosting rounds likely underfit tabular Alpha158 features; valid period early-stopping not fully leveraged; stock IC benefits from more capacity. Decision: Increment to 1000 rounds, lower lr=0.03, increase num_leaves=64 + fractions for better generalization while keeping training fast; rerun train then submit_eval to measure improvement and update time budget estimate.

trajectory.description: Observation: LGB with early-stop (at 59 trees) reached ic_mean=0.0300 (slight gain from 0.0281 baseline); valid MSE plateaus immediately (~0.000722), train time ~102s per iter. Analysis: MSE on noisy next-day returns is poor proxy for IC; early-stop prevents learning useful patterns, under-utilizes 2021 data. Decision: retrain on full 2008-2021 without early-stop (fixed 1000 rounds), lower LR=0.02 + regularization (L1/L2, min_data_in_leaf) for better generalization on tabular Alpha158; keep predict.py unchanged and submit_eval to track IC improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: MSE-optimized LGB (1000 trees, full 2008-2021 train) reached ic_mean=0.0368 (best so far). Analysis: MSE poorly aligned with cross-sectional IC; 2021 data was leaked into training previously; custom IC feval on held-out 2021 valid for early-stopping + more rounds/lower LR will better optimize the target metric and reduce overfitting. Decision: Overwrite train.py to use direct lgb.train() with train=2008-2020/valid=2021 split, custom pearsonr IC metric (higher better) for feval+early_stop(200), 5000 rounds/lr=0.01/num_leaves=64, record evals to metrics.jsonl; run training then submit_eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed with ValueError in ic_eval (arrays not same length) due to feval being called on both train and valid sets (valid_dates only matches valid). Previous best ic_mean=0.0368 with full-train 1000 trees. Analysis: Custom IC feval is correct direction for target metric but needs length guard for train-set calls; MSE not aligned with IC so early-stop on valid IC (2021) should improve test generalization. Decision: Fix ic_eval with length check (dummy for train, real IC for valid), use valid_dates.values, set 2000 rounds/lr=0.02/early_stop=100 on IC, keep logging; rerun train.py then submit_eval.

[TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 5669s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: IC-feval early-stop on 2021 valid underfits (86 trees, ic_mean drops to 0.0319 from best 0.0368); noisy daily IC makes early-stop unreliable. Full-data 1000-tree training aligned better with test IC. Analysis: MSE proxy suboptimal but fixed long training on all labeled data (to 2021) captures more signal from Alpha158; valid IC not stable enough for stopping. Decision: Revise train.py to combined 2008-2021 training (no valid/early-stop), lower lr=0.01 + num_leaves=64 + 2000 rounds + regularization for smoother learning; update logging for train-only metrics.jsonl; run training then submit_eval to target ic_mean >0.0368 within time budget.
